subject: Chemistry
title: TNT: The Standard Explosive
description: A 60-second lesson on TNT: The Standard Explosive.

hook.text: TNT is so stable you can hit it with a hammer and it won't explode. It needs a detonator to go off.

buildup.text: Trinitrotoluene was first made in 1863 by a German chemist. For years, nobody realized it was explosive.

discovery.text: When detonated, TNT decomposes instantly into gases that expand at 6,900 meters per second.

twist.text: It was used as a yellow dye before anyone discovered its explosive power. Imagine dying fabric with TNT.

climax.text: All explosives are now measured in 'tons of TNT equivalent.' It became the universal yardstick of destruction.

punchline.text: First a dye, then a bomb, now a unit of measurement.

quiz.question: What was TNT originally used for before its explosive power was discovered?
